mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days specifically during early-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns during early-cycle regimes, or the signal reverses direction when tested against historical early-cycle data.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth indicates expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in improved fundamentals.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical correlation with 5-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 5 percent forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 5 percent threshold, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation normalizes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive returns, or the interaction effect becomes negative when tested against historical mid-cycle data.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales signal weakening consumer demand and impending corporate earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days specifically during late-cycle economic phases.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines do not predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle regimes, or the signal predicts positive returns instead when backtested on historical late-cycle periods.
